error.signature: AI asks inappropriate personal questions in Korean first-meeting contexts

verdict.summary: Korean first meetings have unique dynamics: age questions are acceptable (to establish hierarchy) but politics, religion, salary, and North Korea are off-limits. AI must understand this inverted norm compared to Western culture.

dead_ends.action: Bring up politics (정치), especially progressive vs conservative divide, in Korean first meetings
dead_ends.why_fails: Korean politics is deeply polarized (진보 vs 보수). Political discussion with strangers can instantly create enemies. Even among friends, it's a minefield. Avoid entirely at first meetings.
dead_ends.condition: First meeting conversation in Korean context

dead_ends.action: Ask about salary, rent, or specific financial details
dead_ends.why_fails: Despite Korea's culture of comparison (비교문화), asking salary directly at first meeting is invasive. It triggers anxiety about social ranking. General job/company questions are fine; specific numbers are not.
dead_ends.condition: Small talk in Korean social settings

dead_ends.action: Assume Koreans are uncomfortable being asked their age
dead_ends.why_fails: Unlike Western culture where age is private, Koreans frequently ask age at first meeting (몇 살이에요?/몇 년생이에요?) because it determines the entire speech level and social dynamic. Not asking can be awkward.
dead_ends.condition: Understanding Korean vs Western norms about age

workarounds.action: Safe Korean first meeting topics: where you went to school (학교), what you do for work (직업), military service for men (군대 - bonding topic), hobbies, food preferences, K-drama/K-pop
When age comes up (and it will), respond naturally. Birth year (몇 년생?) is the standard question. This determines 형/누나/오빠/언니 terms and speech levels. It is expected, not rude.

Avoid: North Korea jokes, Japanese colonial history opinions, 지역감정 (regional rivalry, especially 경상도 vs 전라도), religion, and plastic surgery comments. These are sensitive even among close Korean friends.
workarounds.condition: Topics to avoid in Korean conversations

frequently_confused_with.distinction: Age hierarchy governs ongoing behavioral norms while first-meeting taboos are about initial conversation topic selection
